issues.title: CG stagnation returns a partial solution as if it succeeded
issues.detail: Both linear solvers return the current iterate when `dAd` underflows (`solve_poisson_cg` here and `solve_helmholtz_cg` at the analogous branch later in the file). That turns a numerical breakdown into a success-shaped result, so the MAC step can continue with an invalid pressure or diffusion solve and silently corrupt the flow field.
issues.suggested_fix: Replace the warning-and-return branches with `RuntimeError` (including iteration/residual details) so stagnation is surfaced instead of accepted.

issues.title: Pressure operator is only correct for square cells but rectangular grids are accepted
issues.detail: `solve_poisson_cg` uses a single `inv_h2 = 1.0 / (dx * dy)` factor with an isotropic 5-point stencil. That is only valid when `dx == dy`, but `SolverConfig` allows `nx != ny`, so rectangular grids will silently get the wrong pressure solve.
issues.suggested_fix: Implement the Laplacian with separate `1/dx**2` and `1/dy**2` coefficients, or explicitly validate and reject non-square grids in the public configuration.

issues.title: Latest required checks did not run successfully
issues.detail: The recorded lint, typecheck, and test steps all failed with `command not found` (`ruff`, `mypy`, and `pytest`). In this state the task is not demonstrably complete, and the repository cannot show that existing behavior still passes the required checks.
issues.suggested_fix: Provision the check tools in the project setup/environment (for example by adding the missing dev dependencies and using the project venv in the check commands) and rerun `ruff`, `mypy`, and `pytest` until they pass.
